name: Republic of Genoa (c.1099–1797)
description: A white field charged with a red Greek cross extending to the edges — the Cross of Saint George.
history: The Cross of Saint George on a white field was the flag of the Republic of Genoa from at least the 12th century. Saint George had been the patron saint of Genoa since 958. According to tradition the City of London paid an annual tribute to the Republic of Genoa from 1190 for the privilege of using the same flag (the basis of England's flag). Used continuously until Napoleon dissolved the Republic in 1797.
